analysis.summary: This file implements a collection of loss computation functions for a multi-agent reinforcement learning agent in the LUX AI competition. It handles policy gradient updates, entropy regularization, teacher-student KL divergence, and value/baseline loss calculation. The functions carefully manage multi-unit action masks to ensure gradients are only computed for units that actually executed actions. It provides both standard and alternative implementations to support different training configurations and sampling strategies.
insights: Multi-unit RL requires explicit masking of log-probabilities and entropies to prevent gradient updates for units that did not act.
Conditional probability adjustment is applied when sampling actions without replacement to maintain valid distributions and avoid division by zero.
Teacher-student KL divergence is computed by detaching the teacher policy to isolate the student's learning signal during distillation.
Loss reduction strategies are abstracted to allow flexible aggregation across time, batch, and player dimensions depending on the training phase.
